message:
fix(concurrency): ScannerStore deep-cloning, executor snapshot race, and t.Parallel() removal across 90+ test files

ScannerStore data race fix (scanner.go, +126 lines):
- All public ScannerStore methods now return deep clones via cloneScan()
  and cloneFinding() instead of sharing references to mutex-guarded
  internal records — previously, callers could read/write ScanRecord and
  FindingRecord fields after the store's mutex was released, causing
  data races between the HTTP handler goroutines and the background scan
  runner
- UpdateScan and UpdateFinding changed from replace-by-value to a
  callback pattern (fn func(*ScanRecord)) so mutations happen under the
  lock instead of on a possibly-stale copy
- Deep clone covers Options (Languages, Exclude slices), CompletedAt,
  DismissedAt, and FindingIDs

Executor snapshot race fix (executor.go):
- Move stateMu.RLock() before the nil check in snapshotState() — the
  previous ordering read e.state without holding the lock, racing with
  the Run() goroutine that initializes it under stateMu.Lock()

Pane streamer (pane_streamer.go, +14 lines):
- Additional streaming infrastructure

Test suite (90+ files, -2100 lines):
- Remove t.Parallel() from test functions and subtests across
  internal/pipeline/, internal/robot/, internal/serve/, and
  internal/tui/dashboard/ — continuing the cleanup from the prior
  commit. These tests share process-global state (scanner store
  singletons, tmux stubs, theme globals, pipeline executors) that
  cannot be safely accessed concurrently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -342,6 +342,20 @@ func (ps *PaneStreamer) runFIFOReader() {
 			if err != io.EOF && !strings.Contains(err.Error(), "file already closed") {
 				log.Printf("pipe-pane: read error for %s: %v", ps.target, err)
 			}
+			
+			// Check if we are actually stopping
+			select {
+			case <-stopCh:
+				return
+			case <-ctx.Done():
+				return
+			default:
+				// Unexpected stream termination, fallback to polling
+				log.Printf("pipe-pane: stream ended unexpectedly for %s, switching to fallback", ps.target)
+				ps.useFallback.Store(true)
+				ps.wg.Add(1)
+				go ps.runPollingLoop()
+			}
 			return
 		case line := <-lineCh:
 			lineBuf = append(lineBuf, strings.TrimSuffix(line, "\n"))
